name: Pick-One Upgrade Choice
description: A run reward screen where the player chooses one upgrade from a small generated set and commits it to the current build.
design_notes: Pick-one choices convert randomness into agency. The reward generator should know the current run state, eligible pools, duplicate rules, rarity weights, reroll policy, and preview text before showing choices. The player should understand why each option matters without seeing hidden formulas.
design_purpose: Give players agency over random rewards without eliminating adaptation.
Create build direction through repeated small commitments.
Support synergies, pivots, and scarcity by controlling reward pool eligibility.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are drafting a run-defining build from several tempting but mutually exclusive options.

parameters.name: reward_choice_id
parameters.description: Persistent id for the generated choice set.

parameters.name: reward_pool_id
parameters.description: Source pool filtered before options are drawn.

parameters.name: choice_slot_count
parameters.description: Number of visible options in the choice set.

parameters.name: item_rarity_tier
parameters.description: Rarity tier assigned before or after eligibility filtering.

parameters.name: duplicate_reward_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule for options that overlap with owned upgrades.

parameters.name: reroll_price_curve_id
parameters.description: Price rule used if the player asks for new options.

parameters.name: synergy_bias_profile_id
parameters.description: Weight profile that reacts to current build tags.

edge_cases: All generated options become invalid after another system changes inventory or build tags.
A reroll uses the same reward_choice_id and redraws identical options from the same RNG cursor.
Two options are technically different but share the same visible effect and feel duplicated.
The player closes the reward screen during a networked run before committing or skipping.
An option references an unlock_pool_id that is not available for the current account state.
common_bugs: Selection commits on button down and again on confirm, granting two upgrades.
Preview text uses post-selection state and shows inflated synergy effects.
Reroll price is charged even if reward generation fails and falls back to the old set.
Owned relic filters run after rarity weighting, creating empty rare slots.
Reward choice data is regenerated on reload instead of restoring the persisted offer ids.
balancing_notes: Too much synergy bias makes runs solve themselves; too little makes builds feel disconnected.
Choice slot count changes both decision difficulty and average build power.
Skip rewards should be useful enough to reduce frustration but not so efficient that upgrades are commonly ignored.
accessibility_notes: Show full option text, comparison hints, and keyword explanations without requiring fast reading.
Support controller, keyboard, and screen-reader ordering that matches the visual option order.
Avoid color-only rarity presentation; include labels or shapes for item_rarity_tier.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate reward_choice_id from the run_id and reward event, then draw ScriptableObject upgrade ids through eligibility filters, rarity weights, and duplicate_reward_rule_id. Commit the selected option through one run-state transaction so UI double clicks cannot grant extras.
implementation_notes.godot: Store upgrade definitions as Resources and offered ids in the run save. A Control screen should display restored offers, call a single selection method, and update run tags only after the chosen reward is committed.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets for upgrades and a reward subsystem that can log the RNG stream, selected reward_pool_id, and score breakdown for designers. Networked runs should let the server commit reward_choice_id and replicate the chosen upgrade to clients.
implementation_notes.web: Generate choices from versioned JSON using deterministic RNG streams and persist the offered option ids. Phaser or custom UIs should render from the persisted offer record rather than redrawing on refresh.

relationships.reason: Upgrade choices need deterministic reward pools, rarity weighting, and duplicate prevention.
Choice UI needs readable option focus, comparison text, and commit/cancel behavior.
Temporary run upgrades can blur with permanent skill trees unless persistence boundaries are explicit.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Offers small sets of run upgrades that shape the current build.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses post-combat card choices as a central run-building decision.
